title: auto should be preferred to avoid repetition of types
description: "auto" should be used to avoid repetition of typesAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEcppcoreguidelinessince-c++11clumsyWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen used as a type specifier in a declaration,autoallows the compiler to deduce the type of a variable based on the type of the initialization expression.When the spelling of the initialization expression already contains the type of the declared variable, it leaves no ambiguity andautoshould be used as it makes the code easier to read and reduces duplication. This includes:Initializations usingnewvoid f() { LongAndBoringClassName *avoid = new LongAndBoringClassName(); // Noncompliant auto prefer = new LongAndBoringClassName(); // Compliant }Template factory f
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Use 'auto' to avoid repetition of types in 'new' expressions.
Use 'auto' to avoid repetition of types in 'new' expressions.
Use 'auto' to avoid repetition of types in variable declarations.
Use 'auto' to avoid repetition of namespace-qualified types.
Use 'auto' to avoid repetition of namespace-qualified types in 'new' expressions.
Use 'auto' to avoid repetition of types in smart pointer declarations.
Use 'auto' to avoid repetition of standard container types.
